pr_context.title: Add admin export endpoint and payment multi-method support
pr_context.description: Adds a new /admin/export route, updates payment processing to charge multiple payment methods, and introduces session management helpers.
pr_context.author: dev-user

planner_summary.pr_summary: This PR adds an unauthenticated admin export endpoint, refactors payment processing to use a forEach loop over multiple methods, and adds session/format utilities.

findings.title: Raw user input concatenated into SQL query
findings.description: The search parameter is interpolated directly into a raw SQL string. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL including UNION SELECT to exfiltrate data.
findings.vulnerable_code: const result = await db.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '${search}'`);
findings.recommendation: Use parameterised queries: await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = $1', [search]);

findings.title: Admin export route missing authentication middleware
findings.description: The /admin/export route is registered without requireAuth middleware, making it publicly accessible to any unauthenticated caller.
findings.recommendation: Add requireAuth (and an admin role check) before the handler: router.get('/admin/export', requireAuth, requireRole('admin'), async (req, res) => {

findings.title: JWT signed with hardcoded fallback secret
findings.description: When JWT_SECRET is not set the token is signed with the literal string 'fallback-secret', making tokens forgeable in any environment where the env var is absent.
findings.recommendation: Throw a startup error if JWT_SECRET is undefined: if (!process.env.JWT_SECRET) throw new Error('JWT_SECRET must be set');

planner_focus_validation.focus_area: Missing auth middleware on the new /admin/export route added at line 34 of users.ts
JWT secret appears to be read from process.env without a fallback check in auth.ts

security_results.review_notes: Two critical issues: SQL injection and missing auth. Both must be resolved before merge.

findings.title: forEach does not await async callbacks — rejections silently swallowed
findings.description: paymentMethods.forEach(async ...) does not await each chargeCard call. If chargeCard rejects (e.g. card declined), the rejection becomes an unhandled promise rejection and crashes the process in Node 15+.
findings.test_case: Mock chargeCard to reject with PaymentError for the second element. Expected: processPayment rejects. Actual: resolves silently.

findings.title: while loop has no iteration guard — hangs indefinitely if payment never confirms
findings.description: The while (!paymentConfirmed) loop has no maximum attempt count or timeout. If checkPaymentStatus() always returns false (e.g. provider outage), the process hangs forever.
findings.test_case: Mock checkPaymentStatus to always return false. Expected: throws after MAX_ATTEMPTS. Actual: hangs indefinitely.

planner_focus_validation.focus_area: The new retry loop at line 89 has no maximum iteration guard
Promise.all on line 102 will reject entirely if one payment method fails
planner_focus_validation.note: No Promise.all present in this diff. The planner likely flagged a concern that does not exist in the current code.

findings.title: createSession has 6 positional parameters — use an options object
findings.current_code: async function createSession(userId: string, role: string, expiresIn: number, rememberMe: boolean, ipAddress: string, userAgent: string)
findings.suggestion:
interface CreateSessionOptions { userId: string; role: string; expiresIn: number; rememberMe: boolean; ipAddress: string; userAgent: string; }
async function createSession(options: CreateSessionOptions): Promise<Session>
findings.rationale: Positional parameters force callers to remember order. An options object makes call sites self-documenting and allows safe addition of future fields.

findings.title: fmtDt parameter typed as any — use Date
findings.rationale: any bypasses the type checker. The function only calls .toISOString() which exists on Date — use Date as the parameter type and rename for clarity.

findings.title: Comment '// increment counter' restates the code — remove it
findings.rationale: The comment adds no information beyond what the code already says.

praise.note: formatCurrency correctly uses Intl.NumberFormat with an explicit locale and currency parameter, making it internationalisation-safe rather than hardcoding dollar signs.
